dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal specifies 6-month lookback period. Mapped to sma_200 (approximately 6 months of trading days: 252/4 ≈ 63 days, closest available is sma_200 as a proxy for medium-term trend). Monthly rebalance enforced via hold_days_max of 21 days. Direction is context-dependent: long when close > MA, short when close < MA. Exit conditions implemented as crosses to handle reversion to moving average.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal specifies 6-month lookback period. Mapped to sma_200 (approximately 6 months of trading days: 252/4 ≈ 63 days, closest available is sma_200 as a proxy for medium-term trend). Monthly rebalance enforced via hold_days_max of 21 days. Direction is context-dependent: long when close > MA, short when close < MA. Exit conditions implemented as crosses to handle reversion to moving average.

source_rule_text: A trader should take long positions when price is above its moving average and short positions when price is below it, using a fixed lookback period to identify trend direction in futures markets.
